reasoning:
The resolution essentially hinges on whether at least 1 'Violence against civilians' event occurs in each respective 30-day window prior to the resolution date. The threshold to surpass is the 360-day average preceding 2025-03-16, computationally given as 0.167. Because ACLED counts are integers, any period with 1 or more events resolves as YES (1 > 0.167), and 0 events resolves as NO. 
The historical base rate is ~2 events per year, making the probability of >=1 event in any given 30-day period approximately 15% (via a Poisson distribution where λ = 0.167). 
1) For 2025-03-23, we are already at March 16. Thus, 23 days of the 30-day window have passed. Extensive news searches show no recent reporting of any violence against civilians in Gambia. Considering only 7 days remain, the probability drops considerably to ~6%. 
2) For 2025-04-15, the period is almost entirely in the future. The expectation sits near the 15% base rate. 
3) For subsequent dates, predicting further into the future requires increasing uncertainty towards 0.5. As we approach 2026, the pre-election climate (ahead of the expected late 2026 presidential election) may also introduce slight upward pressure to the baseline risk. Hence, predictions rise progressively from 19% to 29%.
